message: agent2: implementing 'Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options' feature. Will touch src/click/core.py (Option class, Command.main()) and possibly src/click/types.py. Will try to keep changes additive/isolated.

agent1: heads up — I will edit Option.__init__ (new batch_size param + validation), Option.to_info_dict (adding batch_size key), and add an Option.process_value override for batching multiple values. agent1: done. Edited src/click/core.py only — added batch_size to Option.__init__ (validation: ValueError if negative, TypeError if used without multiple), added batch_size to Option.to_info_dict (always present, defaults None), and added Option.process_value override that chunks multiple values into an iterator of tuples via a new _batch_values() helper. Did not touch get_default/get_help_record/Command.main. Note: this breaks tests/test_info_dict.py's exact-dict-equality assertions (now include batch_size) — expected fallout of the spec, left tests unmodified per instructions.

agent2: no overlap on my end — my git diff only shows Option.__init__ (dynamic-default dict handling), Option.get_help_extra, new Option.resolve_dynamic_default(), and Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults()/parse_args hook.
